common.tagline: "When the circus takes time to find itself"
common.footer.copy: CUAC 2026 · May 22–24 · Albergue Nueva Tierra, Piñuecar (Madrid) · cuacvencion@gmail.com
common.footer.legal: Organised by the Spanish circus community. Non-profit.
landing.hero.edition: Second edition · May 2026 · Piñuecar, Sierra Norte de Madrid
landing.hero.countdown.headline: Will you miss it? Only…
landing.gala.title: Circus Gala at Teatro Circo Price
landing.gala.posterAlt: CUAC Circus Gala poster at Teatro Circo Price, 29 March 2026
landing.gala.body1: To support the social project of the Universal Circus Convention, we are hosting a grand circus gala at one of Madrid's most iconic venues.
landing.gala.body2: A show for all audiences and ages. Come enjoy the best contemporary circus and help make CUAC possible.
landing.highlights.title: What awaits you at CUAC?
landing.highlights.desc: Four days of circus in the heart of nature. No competition, no hierarchies. Just practice, community and time to truly connect with the circus.
landing.highlights.0.title: Circus workshops
landing.highlights.0.desc: Led by young artists from all over Spain. All disciplines, all levels.
landing.highlights.1.title: Free training
landing.highlights.1.desc: Spaces open 24h for free practice, creation and personal research.
landing.highlights.2.title: Big top cabaret
landing.highlights.2.desc: Nights of participative cabaret. The intimate stage where mistakes and experiments are welcome.
landing.highlights.3.title: Circus community
landing.highlights.3.desc: Living together with young circus artists from across the country. Meals, conversations, real connections.
landing.highlights.4.title: Nature and mountains
landing.highlights.4.desc: At Albergue Nueva Tierra, Piñuecar. Meadows, silence and clean air of the Sierra Norte de Madrid.
landing.highlights.5.title: Open closing gala
landing.highlights.5.desc: Final show open to the public. The circus leaves the tent and reaches the whole community.
landing.experience.body1: CUAC is not your typical festival. It's a living convention: accommodation, training, meals and nights under the tent, all in one place. The Sierra Norte de Madrid sets the stage; you bring the circus.
landing.experience.body2: Specialised workshops during the day, participative cabarets at night and free training at any time. No pressure. No mandatory productivity. The process always comes before the product.
landing.values.title: The values that drive us
landing.values.desc: CUAC does not compete or beg: it convenes. Every piece of communication breathes at least one of these pillars.
landing.values.0.name: Community
landing.values.0.desc: Building real connections between circus artists from all over Spain.
landing.values.1.name: Horizontality
landing.values.1.desc: Shared learning without hierarchies or competition.
landing.values.2.name: Time
landing.values.2.desc: Respect for natural rhythms. No rush, no pressure.
landing.values.3.name: Nature
landing.values.3.desc: Circus in harmony with the environment. Mountains, meadow and silence.
landing.values.4.name: Diversity
landing.values.4.desc: All ages, levels and circus disciplines.
landing.values.5.name: Dignification
landing.values.5.desc: Contemporary circus as a first-order art form in Spain.
landing.collaborators.title: Partner schools and collectives
landing.collaborators.desc: CUAC is built by everyone together. Thanks to the schools, companies and collectives that make this gathering possible.
landing.channels.title: Follow us and join in
landing.channels.desc: CUAC is community. Share, comment and join the conversation.
landing.tickets.doubts: Any questions? Write to us at
donde.hero.title: We're heading to the mountains
donde.hero.desc: CUAC 2026 takes place at Albergue Nueva Tierra, in Piñuecar, in the heart of the Sierra Norte de Madrid. A space surrounded by nature where the circus can be what it wants to be.
donde.venue.title: Albergue Nueva Tierra
donde.venue.hectares: 10 hectares · Sierra del Rincón Biosphere Reserve
donde.venue.what.title: What does this space offer?
donde.venue.what.0: Space to set up the big top
donde.venue.what.2: Accommodation and community living in the same place
donde.venue.what.3: Meadows, ash trees, oaks and great biodiversity
donde.venue.what.5: Freedom to extend training sessions, talks and nights
donde.transport.title: Choose how to get here
donde.transport.desc: Piñuécar is well connected to Madrid. Here are your three options for getting here.
donde.transport.car.title: By car
donde.transport.car.desc: The exact address is C. Alta, 26, 28737 Piñuécar, Madrid (Albergue Nueva Tierra).
donde.transport.bus.title: By public transport
donde.transport.bus.desc:
Depart from Plaza de Castilla interchange (Madrid).

🗓 Weekends: Line 196 goes direct to Piñuécar (~2 h, departure 9:05).
💼 Weekdays: Line 191 to Buitrago del Lozoya (~90 min), then transfer to 191B to Piñuécar.

Operator: ALSA/CRTM.
donde.transport.blablacuac.title: BlaBlaCUAC
donde.transport.blablacuac.desc: Looking to share a ride? Get in touch and we'll add you to the WhatsApp group to coordinate with other attendees.
donde.environment.title: Sierra del Rincón
cuac.hero.title: The gathering the circus needed
cuac.hero.desc: From 22 to 24 May 2026, the Universal Convention of Circus Arts returns to the Sierra Norte of Madrid. Three days of circus, coexistence and community without performance or production pressure.
cuac.intro.title: A convention for young Spanish circus
cuac.quote: "Less noise, more connection. Less rush, more process."
cuac.pillars.title: Six principles that define CUAC
cuac.pillars.0.title: Coexistence
cuac.pillars.0.desc: Not a consumer festival. Accommodation, meals and training share the same space.
cuac.pillars.1.title: Horizontal practice
cuac.pillars.1.desc: Workshops, free spaces and collective training without hierarchies or silos.
cuac.pillars.2.title: Circus nights
cuac.pillars.2.desc: Participative cabarets and open scenes that emerge organically, without rigid programming.
cuac.pillars.3.title: Nature
cuac.pillars.3.desc: The Sierra Norte de Madrid as the stage: space, silence and the rhythm the body needs.
cuac.pillars.4.title: Process, not product
cuac.pillars.4.desc: The focus is on the shared journey. No performance pressure or constant display.
cuac.pillars.5.title: National vision
cuac.pillars.5.desc: A transversal meeting point where different generations and disciplines converge on equal terms.
convivencia.hero.title: Coexistence
convivencia.hero.desc: The CUACvención is a festival created to share, enjoy and grow as a community. To make this possible, we have a serious coexistence protocol that guarantees a safe, respectful and healthy environment.
convivencia.values.title: Community as the first pillar
convivencia.values.desc: CUAC doesn't just manage bodies in motion: it manages people, energies and real coexistence. Everything we do stems from a shared commitment to mutual care.
convivencia.values.0.title: Respect
convivencia.values.0.desc: Towards people, spaces and the nature that hosts us.
convivencia.values.1.title: Safety
convivencia.values.1.desc: A serious protocol that guarantees an environment free from aggression and violence.
convivencia.values.2.title: Care
convivencia.values.2.desc: Of oneself and the collective. We take care of each other.
convivencia.values.3.title: Joy
convivencia.values.3.desc: Following these guidelines is what makes a memorable gathering possible for everyone.
convivencia.rules.title: Coexistence guidelines
convivencia.rules.desc: These rules are not just a reminder: they are the foundation for us to live together in harmony and fully enjoy the CUACvención.
convivencia.rules.0: Respect all attendees regardless of their background.
convivencia.rules.1: Take care of the environment: do not litter, use the bathroom facilities, dispose of food waste and recycling in the designated containers, and avoid using glass.
convivencia.rules.2: Do not make fire outside the designated hours and area. Camping gas stoves are not allowed in the event area.
convivencia.rules.3: Do not bring pets: they cannot access the training or performance areas. If you cannot leave them in someone else's care, keep them on a lead at all times and always clean up after them.
convivencia.rules.4: Keep communal areas clean, wash up whatever you dirty in the designated area, and leave the bathrooms as you would like to find them.
convivencia.rules.5: Respect the silence and night rest of those sharing the sleeping areas with you.
convivencia.rules.6: Take care of your health and that of those with you: stay well hydrated, eat well, use sunscreen and consume in moderation.
convivencia.rules.7: Always follow the instructions of the volunteer staff on safety matters, both in free training and workshops; follow the instructions of those leading them, respect the registration order and queues for shows.
convivencia.rules.8: No form of violence, aggression or theft will be tolerated. In case of any conflict, contact the care point or the event staff.
convivencia.rules.9: ENJOY, SHARE, PLAY, DANCE, LAUGH, LEARN AND MAKE THIS EVENT A WONDERFUL PLACE.
convivencia.rules.footer: The organisation reserves the right to take appropriate action in case of non-compliance with these rules. Thank you for your cooperation.
convivencia.care.title: A space to care and be cared for
convivencia.care.intro: The Care Point is a physical or roving space made up of visible, identifiable people who accompany possible conflicts during the convention, acting from intersectionality, restorative justice and collective care.
convivencia.care.principles.0.title: Intersectionality
convivencia.care.principles.0.desc: Violations are shaped by axes of oppression such as gender, age, race, class or body. We recognise these intersections to avoid simplistic responses.
convivencia.care.principles.1.title: Transfeminist lens
convivencia.care.principles.1.desc: We take a transfeminist stance that denaturalises gender inequalities, centring consent, autonomy and mutual care.
convivencia.care.principles.2.title: Restorative justice
convivencia.care.principles.2.desc: We address conflicts through accountability, repair and transformation, not punishment. The goal is to get to the root of harm to prevent it from recurring.
convivencia.care.principles.3.title: Horizontality
convivencia.care.principles.3.desc: We reject all imposed authority. Decisions are made collectively. Managing harm is everyone's shared responsibility.
convivencia.care.principles.4.title: Care as a core practice
convivencia.care.principles.4.desc: Care is not an add-on — it is the seed of our practice. Emotionally supporting those going through difficult situations is a shared responsibility.
convivencia.care.intervention.title: Intervention protocol
convivencia.care.intervention.desc: When harm or violence occurs, we act carefully, ensuring safety, validating the person harmed and offering a space for accountability.
convivencia.care.steps.0: Ensure immediate safety. Separate the parties involved to prevent the situation from escalating or recurring.
convivencia.care.steps.1: Support the person who experienced harm. Listen with attention and respect, without minimising or questioning what happened. Respect their pace and autonomy.
convivencia.care.steps.2: Name what happened. Make the harm visible without definitively labelling the person who caused it. Acknowledging the harm is the first step towards accountability.
convivencia.care.steps.3: Support the person who caused harm. Create a space for them to acknowledge the harm and engage in an accountability process, knowing that behaviour can change.
convivencia.care.steps.4: Safety measures. If the person who caused harm shows no willingness to be accountable, the Care Point will take appropriate action, including possible removal from the event.
convivencia.care.steps.5: Tailored responses. Each situation is different and requires responses tailored to the needs of those involved. We do not follow closed or standard procedures.
convivencia.care.intervention.note: This protocol is based on materials from A Melonera, Kampas and the Colectivo de circo Molino Rojo, as well as projects like Cirkolepsia and Cameros Cuir.
convivencia.cta.title: The process before the product
convivencia.cta.body1: CUAC understands circus as an artistic, pedagogical and community practice. This means that taking care of the environment in which we practise is as important as the quality of the training.
convivencia.cta.body2: We believe in a space where body, mind and community feel at ease. For any question, issue or improvement suggestion, do not hesitate to write to us.
colaborar.hero.title: CUAC is looking for collaborators
colaborar.hero.desc: For the project to come together and everything to go as planned, we need you — if you want to help — to join us. We are several organisations keeping this alive.
colaborar.why.title: A project for everyone
colaborar.why.body1: CUAC is not built from the top: it is built from the community. Communication, volunteering, programming, logistics, technical production… every area needs people with enthusiasm and talent.
colaborar.why.body2: If you want to be part of this gathering — not just as an attendee, but as an active part that makes it possible — this is your place. Write to us and we will tell you in more detail how you fit in.
colaborar.benefits.0.title: Learn by doing
colaborar.benefits.0.desc: Event production, cultural communication and community management from the inside.
colaborar.benefits.1.title: Be part of the circus
colaborar.benefits.1.desc: Full festival access, training, communal living and being part of the community.
colaborar.benefits.2.title: Build something real
colaborar.benefits.2.desc: CUAC is born from the commitment of those who believe circus deserves this space.
colaborar.areas.title: How can you help?
colaborar.areas.0.title: Communication
colaborar.areas.0.desc: Social media, graphic design, photography, video and festival promotion.
colaborar.areas.1.title: Volunteering
colaborar.areas.1.desc: On-site support: accreditations, setup, space management.
colaborar.areas.2.title: Programming
colaborar.areas.2.desc: Workshop proposals, artist coordination and schedule design.
colaborar.areas.3.title: Logistics and catering
colaborar.areas.3.desc: Community meal management, shopping and hostel provisioning.
colaborar.areas.4.title: Technical production
colaborar.areas.4.desc: Tent setup, sound, lighting and training spaces.
colaborar.areas.5.title: What you bring
colaborar.areas.5.desc: If you have talent, enthusiasm and energy, there is a place for you at CUAC.
colaborar.colabs.title: Those who already trust CUAC
colaborar.cta.title: Do you want to join this family?
contacto.hero.title: Contact
contacto.hero.desc: Do you have questions, want to collaborate or simply want to know more? We are on social media and on email. Write to us.
contacto.channels.title: Find us here
contacto.channels.desc: CUAC is community. Share, comment and join the conversation.
contacto.channels.tiktok.desc: Short videos, highlights and moments from the gathering.
contacto.channels.whatsapp.desc: Quick alerts and last-minute schedule updates.
contacto.channels.email.desc: For collaborations, press or any enquiry.
contacto.cta.title: Have something to tell us?
contacto.cta.body: Whether you want to collaborate, propose an activity, resolve a query or simply say hello, send us an email. We try to respond within 48 hours.
contacto.faq.title: Frequently asked questions
contacto.faq.desc: Here are the questions we get most often. If you can't find what you're looking for, write to us.
contacto.faq.stillQuestions: Can't find what you're looking for? Write to us at
contacto.faq.cat0.label: Tickets & access
contacto.faq.cat0.q0: Can I come for just one day?
contacto.faq.cat0.a0: Yes. There will be a day pass for €20.40, valid for Friday, Saturday or Sunday.
contacto.faq.cat0.q1: What types of ticket are available and how much do they cost?
contacto.faq.cat0.a1: The general ticket costs €61.20 and includes full access for all 3 days, galas, cabarets and the camping area. The youth ticket (ages 7–15) costs €30.60. The day pass costs €20.40. Children under 6 are free.
contacto.faq.cat0.q2: Can tickets be purchased at the event?
contacto.faq.cat0.a2: Yes. General tickets, day passes and gala tickets can all be purchased at the on-site ticket point during the event.
contacto.faq.cat0.q3: Does the ticket include access to the showers?
contacto.faq.cat0.a3: Yes. The ticket includes shower access, not only for those staying in dormitory rooms.
contacto.faq.cat0.q4: How does arrival and check-in work?
contacto.faq.cat0.a4: On arrival we will validate your ticket, give you your wristband or pass and direct you to the right area.
contacto.faq.cat0.q5: Is there a single entrance to the site?
contacto.faq.cat0.a5: Yes. The main access is through a single controlled entry point.
contacto.faq.cat0.q6: Is the site enclosed or open like a village?
contacto.faq.cat0.a6: The event is held in an enclosed, perimeter-fenced hostel. You can leave the site at any time, but the event space has defined boundaries.
contacto.faq.cat0.q7: Do the general ticket and day passes include access to galas and cabarets?
contacto.faq.cat0.a7: Yes, the general ticket, youth tickets and day passes all include access to galas and cabarets.
contacto.faq.cat0.q8: Can I put up my tent in the camping area with the general ticket?
contacto.faq.cat0.a8: Yes, the camping area is included with the general ticket at no extra cost.
contacto.faq.cat1.label: Getting there
contacto.faq.cat1.q0: How do I get to the gathering?
contacto.faq.cat1.a0: You can get there by bus from Madrid or by carpooling. Find all the transport details on our getting there page.
contacto.faq.cat1.q1: Is there an organised ridesharing system?
contacto.faq.cat1.a1: Yes. We will have an internal carpooling group called BlaBlaCUAC. You can find all the details on our getting there page.
contacto.faq.cat1.a2: Yes. There will be a designated parking area inside the site.
contacto.faq.cat2.label: Accommodation & camping
contacto.faq.cat2.q0: What is the camping area like?
contacto.faq.cat2.a0: The camping area is inside the hostel grounds, in a natural setting with shade and close to the showers and other services.
contacto.faq.cat2.q1: Are there sleeping options beyond camping?
contacto.faq.cat2.a1: Yes. You can come with a tent, in a van, or book a bed in a shared dormitory. The hostel does not have private rooms.
contacto.faq.cat2.q2: If I come with a group, can we try to sleep together?
contacto.faq.cat2.a2: Yes. We will do our best to place groups together when requested at booking. If you want to be sure of sharing a room, we recommend getting in touch in advance.
contacto.faq.cat2.q3: Can I arrive the day before to set up camp?
contacto.faq.cat2.a3: Yes, as long as you let us know a little in advance. If you can help with setup, we'd really appreciate it.
contacto.faq.cat3.label: Workshops, gala & activities
contacto.faq.cat3.q0: I've never done circus before — are there beginner workshops?
contacto.faq.cat3.a0: Yes. There will be plenty of introductory sessions designed for people who have never tried circus before.
contacto.faq.cat3.q1: What kind of workshops will there be?
contacto.faq.cat3.a1: There will be workshops in juggling, acrobatics, aerial silks and other aerials, diabolo, poi, acro-yoga, partnering, balancing and rola-bola, among others.
contacto.faq.cat3.q2: How do I sign up for workshops?
contacto.faq.cat3.a2: Sign-ups will open in time slots: morning workshops open in the morning, afternoon workshops open later in the day. Each day will follow a similar pattern.
contacto.faq.cat3.q3: If I arrive on Friday night or Saturday, can I still sign up for workshops?
contacto.faq.cat3.a3: Yes. You can join and sign up for available workshops as soon as you arrive.
contacto.faq.cat3.q4: If a workshop has already started, can I still join?
contacto.faq.cat3.a4: Yes, as long as there are spots left and the workshop leader agrees it is workable.
contacto.faq.cat3.q5: Can I come just for the gala?
contacto.faq.cat3.a5: Yes. You can buy single-day tickets to attend only the galas and cabarets. Check the full programme on our programme page.
contacto.faq.cat3.q6: How much does the gala cost?
contacto.faq.cat3.a6: Single-day gala and cabaret tickets are sold on Entradium, where the up-to-date price for each session is shown. They can also be purchased on-site at the event's ticket point.
contacto.faq.cat3.q7: Where does the cabaret take place?
contacto.faq.cat3.a7: The cabaret is held in the main tent, the same space used for the gala and other activities.
contacto.faq.cat3.q8: Where will the fire jam be?
contacto.faq.cat3.a8: The fire jam will take place inside the site, in a designated fire area.
contacto.faq.cat4.label: Food, drink & kitchen
contacto.faq.cat4.q0: Will there be food on site, or do I need to bring my own?
contacto.faq.cat4.a0: There will be several options: a budget menu from the hostel kitchen, the option to bring your own food, and a bar in the village — though we cannot guarantee its opening hours.
contacto.faq.cat4.q1: If I don't book meals when buying my ticket, can I buy them on site?
contacto.faq.cat4.a1: Possibly, but we can't guarantee it. It will depend on how well the hostel kitchen can accommodate extra demand.
contacto.faq.cat4.a2: Yes. There will be hobs and a microwave.
contacto.faq.cat4.q3: Can I bring a camping stove?
contacto.faq.cat4.a3: Yes, but it may only be used in designated areas. It will not be allowed next to tents or in any unauthorised zone.
contacto.faq.cat4.a5: Yes. There will be some gluten-free options.
contacto.faq.cat5.label: Families, accessibility & welfare
contacto.faq.cat5.a0: Yes. The space is adapted for access with wheelchairs, pushchairs and prams.
contacto.faq.cat5.a1: Yes. Activities are designed to welcome families and kids. The exception is fire-related activities and the renegade, which are for adults over 18.
contacto.faq.cat5.q2: Do young children need a ticket?
contacto.faq.cat5.a2: Children under 6 are free. The youth ticket (ages 7–15) costs €30.60. From age 16, the general ticket applies.
contacto.faq.cat5.q3: If I come with young children, will they have a wristband or ID?
contacto.faq.cat5.a3: Yes. Children can wear an ID tag to link them to their parent or carer in case it is needed.
contacto.faq.cat5.q4: Will there be a welfare point or safe space?
contacto.faq.cat5.a4: Yes. There will be a welfare point with visible protocols. You can read more on our community guidelines page.
contacto.faq.cat6.label: Rules, community & participation
contacto.faq.cat6.q0: Can I take photos and post them online?
contacto.faq.cat6.a0: Yes. You are welcome to take photos and share them. If you post online, please tag CUAC.
contacto.faq.cat6.q1: Can I bring a small portable speaker?
contacto.faq.cat6.a1: Yes, as long as you use it with consideration for people who may be resting. At night there will also be music spaces with DJs.
contacto.faq.cat6.a2: No. Hostel rules and the nature of the setting mean animals are not permitted on site.
contacto.faq.cat6.a3: Yes, although many spots may already be filled. If you are interested in volunteering, visit our collaboration page or write to us at cuacvencion@gmail.com.
contacto.faq.cat6.q4: How can I help out at the event?
contacto.faq.cat6.a4: Beyond formal volunteering, you can offer help directly at the event. Find all the ways to get involved on our collaboration page.
contacto.faq.cat7.label: Other details
contacto.faq.cat7.q0: What will the general atmosphere be like?
contacto.faq.cat7.a0: It will be an open, community-led gathering designed for living together, practising circus and enjoying nature.
contacto.faq.cat7.a1: Yes. There will be T-shirts, tote bags and other keepsakes from the gathering.
contacto.faq.cat7.q2: Will there be a swimming pool?
contacto.faq.cat7.a2: No, there are no plans for a swimming pool.
landing.tickets.title: Tickets are now on sale!
landing.tickets.dates: May 22, 23 and 24, 2026 · Albergue Nueva Tierra, Piñuecar (Madrid)
landing.tickets.step1.title: Choose your living convention ticket
landing.tickets.step1.intro: If you are joining the full living convention, these are the base access options. All living convention tickets include the galas and cabarets.
landing.tickets.step1.general: General admission — €61.20. Full access to the gathering for all 3 days + galas & cabarets + camping area.
landing.tickets.step1.youth: Youth 7–15 years — €30.60. Same as general for those aged 7 to 15.
landing.tickets.step1.dayPass: Day pass — €20.40. If you can only come on Friday, Saturday or Sunday.
landing.tickets.step2.title: Choose your accommodation
landing.tickets.step2.intro: The ticket does not include a bed. Add what you need:
landing.tickets.step2.tent: Camping tent — included in the general ticket.
landing.tickets.step2.hostel: Hostel bed — €20.40 per night (Fri→Sat or Sat→Sun). Shared rooms with communal showers.
landing.tickets.step2.van: Van or vehicle — €10.20. Required if you plan to sleep in your van or car.
landing.tickets.step3.title: Add your meals
landing.tickets.step3.intro: There is a menu for every moment of the day, with vegan and non-vegan options:
landing.tickets.step3.meals: Lunch and dinner — vegan and non-vegan options available.
landing.tickets.step3.availability: Available for Friday, Saturday and Sunday separately.
landing.tickets.step3.note: Meals will be paid for during the event, but we need an estimate in advance of how many people plan to use this service so we can organise properly.
landing.tickets.sessions.title: Day tickets for galas and cabarets
landing.tickets.sessions.body: If you are not attending the full living convention, you can also buy access only to each day's shows. Entradium keeps availability and pricing updated on each link.
landing.tickets.sessions.0.title: Cabaret and fire gala
landing.tickets.sessions.1.title: Cabaret and grand gala
landing.tickets.sessions.2.title: School gala
program.hero.title: CUAC 2026 agenda
program.hero.desc: Three days of workshops, training, shows and community life in Pinuecar.
program.schedule.title: Full program
program.schedule.desc: Preliminary activity distribution. Times may be adjusted due to production needs or weather.
program.item.showFriday13: ¡Qué magia! Yo y mis Circunstancias. Cía Joaco Showman
program.item.showSaturday17: Los hijos del desguace. Fratelli Chatarra
program.detail.registrations.desc: Reception, accreditation and first contacts between artists, schools and attendees.
program.detail.workshopsFri1.desc: Open space where disciplines such as juggling, acrobatics, balancing and aerials coexist. This block is the core of the event: horizontal learning, technical exchange and collective training.
program.detail.showFriday13.desc: A show blending clown and Close-Up/Manipulation magic. It brings the tenderness of clown, paying homage to silent film actors while keeping the gift of speech, with the added value of magic's inherent surprise. Our character sets out to work like any other day, but finds himself forced to put on the show himself when the scheduled company cannot make it. He will have to find a way out of this predicament.
program.detail.workshopsFri2.desc: Continuation of the training space, reinforcing the convention's logic as an open laboratory.
program.detail.showFriday19.desc: A contemporary circus show fusing dance, theatre, juggling and live music. Where being surprised is a game. PARANOIA comes on its own, it is not called. PARANOIA is good, not bad. PARANOIA loves CHIQUITA BANANA. PARANOIA dies and is reborn. PARANOIA has a message.
program.detail.cabaret.desc: Variety show featuring national and international artists. A classic convention format: short numbers, aesthetic variety and a space for hybrid and emerging proposals.
program.detail.fireGala.desc:
One of the most powerful moments of the day: the Fire Gala. A space where fire goes beyond visual impact and becomes a shared artistic language. Body, technique, risk, presence and music come together to create a breathtaking gala.

This year we have a spectacular cast of artists, with very different disciplines, styles and ways of inhabiting the stage. And that is precisely what makes this gala special: there is no single type of fire, nor a single way of telling it.

There is manipulation, dance, juggling, magic and plenty of duende.

A gala made by fire artists for an audience that knows how to watch, feel and celebrate circus when it is alive.
program.detail.fireJamFri.desc: Open night space where artists share, improvise and experiment with fire in a community setting, with Tecla DJing during the jam. Instagram: @tecladej.
program.detail.conciertoRotx.desc:
Rotx is a singer-songwriter music project from the Sierra de Madrid. Style: cantautora, rock-rumba. In this format she will be joined by Gabriel Estévez on percussion.

Her songs deal with social issues, everyday situations, what we see and feel. They are strongly committed to the idea of creating a better world, inspiring and raising awareness. Her music draws from diverse influences including rock, flamenco and Latin American singer-songwriter traditions.

Instagram: @rotx.cantautora
Spotify & YouTube: Rotx Cantautora
program.detail.djFridayRayov.desc: Dj_Rayov soundtracks Friday's fire jam with a progressive set, moving from hypnotic ambience into psy tribe, adapted for fire manipulation. Flow art + contact.
program.detail.workshopsSat1.desc: Continuation of technical and artistic work in community.
program.detail.volleyMaza.desc: An emblematic juggling convention activity: a collective game mixing sport and circus technique.
program.detail.showSaturday13.desc: Menudo emBOLAdo is born from a reunion between Darwin and Sergius, two old friends who haven't seen each other for 5 years. Darwin's plan is to surprise Sergius mid-show to bring back old memories — a surprise that will land Sergius in a real pickle he'll have to get out of as best he can. A show full of humour, balancing and juggling that entertains while prompting reflection on certain aspects of today's society.
program.detail.showSaturday17.desc: Los hijos del desguace by Fratelli Chatarra is part of the open programme in Piñuecar's Plaza del Pueblo.
program.detail.cuacBattle.desc: Is the CUAC Battle a competition? A show? Circus? It's the ultimate show and anything goes! The word "Battle" was not chosen lightly: 8 participants from different disciplines will take to the stage and face off in 30-second rounds, ready to blow the crowd away with style, creativity and fighting spirit. In the end, it's all about convincing the audience and the judges that their tricks, originality and routines deserve the win. Only one can remain!
program.detail.aerialBattle.desc:
Back for another edition, the most anticipated event of the year — the Aerial Battle, where legends are forged. An untamed format blending spectacle, pop culture references, Yellow Humour, Ninja Warrior and a serious acrobatic competition.

You will form teams of 3 aerialists and compete across different elements, facing challenges that will test every one of your skills, with one sole jury: the audience. Two teams enter, one leaves. Win over the crowd and you could claim victory.

Suitable for all levels. Don't hold back, join in.
program.detail.workshopsSat2.desc: The last major intensive training block.
program.detail.grandGala.desc: Grand Circus Gala, the peak of the scenic programme, featuring nationally and internationally acclaimed artists. Expect aerial acts, juggling, Cyr wheel, antipodism and knives, among others. A vibrant and moving show designed for circus lovers and newcomers alike.
program.detail.fireJamSat.desc: A second night dedicated to fire, consolidating this language as one of the convention's main threads, with Tecla DJing during the jam. Instagram: @tecladej.
program.detail.renegade.desc: An alternative night space where the audience can actively participate, proposing challenges and improvised scenic dynamics.
program.detail.djSaturdayDeli.desc: La_Deli from Fratelli Chatarra closes Saturday night with a jumpy 140 bpm techno set.
program.detail.workshopsSun.desc: Last open sessions before the closing ceremony.
program.detail.schoolGala.desc: A space dedicated to students and educational processes, highlighting pedagogical work and the next generation of circus.
program.detail.circolympics.desc: A collective and playful activity that reinforces the community spirit of the event.
program.detail.closing.desc: The symbolic closing of the gathering, a juggling convention tradition, bringing three days of circus community life to an end.
program.note.title: A living community agenda
program.note.body: CUAC keeps the schedule open to adjustments to respect rhythms, weather and technical needs of the circus community.
program.note.whatsappPrompt: To stay updated on schedule changes, join our
meta.landing.title: CUAC 2026 — Universal Convention of Circus Arts
meta.landing.description: 22–24 May at Albergue Nueva Tierra, Sierra Norte de Madrid. Workshops, free training, cabaret under the big top and circus community.
meta.cuac.title: About CUAC — The gathering circus needed
meta.cuac.description: CUAC is the live-together convention for young Spanish circus. Specialist workshops, free practice and participatory cabaret nights.
meta.donde.title: How to get to CUAC 2026 — Albergue Nueva Tierra, Piñuecar
meta.donde.description: CUAC 2026 takes place at Albergue Nueva Tierra, Piñuecar, Sierra Norte de Madrid. Access information by car, bus and train.
meta.convivencia.title: Community guidelines — CUAC 2026
meta.convivencia.description: CUAC coexistence guidelines: community, respect, care for the environment and shared space during the 3-day convention.
meta.colaborar.title: Collaborate with CUAC 2026 — Join the team
meta.colaborar.description: Be part of the team that makes CUAC possible. Volunteering, artistic collaboration, sponsorship and logistical support.
meta.program.title: CUAC 2026 schedule — Preliminary agenda
meta.program.description: Check the preliminary CUAC 2026 schedule: workshops, free training, shows, cabaret and closing events.
meta.contacto.title: Contact CUAC 2026
meta.contacto.description: Reach us on Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, WhatsApp or email. The CUAC team will reply within 48 hours.
